data.title: New Zealand Shares Fall 1.2% on Broad-Based Weakness — Market Talk
data.description: New Zealand's NZX-50 closed 1.2% lower at 12880.36, dragged to its largest one-day fall in more than two weeks from broad-based weakness. Shares of utilities, aged-care providers, property developers and consumer-related companies all finished lower as investors digested a raft of earnings reports and the RBNZ's move to cut the country's cash rate on Wednesday. Casino operator SkyCity led losses, tumbling 7.5% after releasing its December-half results. Local shares of Australian banks Westpac and ANZ lost 5.0% and 4.1%, respectively, mirroring falls in their domestic markets. Precinct Properties shed 2.0% following the release of its interim results. The NZX-50 is down 0.8% so far this week and on course for a sixth weekly loss in eight weeks. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Australia's Hastings partners with billionaire Andrew Forrest's Wyloo; shares rise
data.description:
** Shares of Hastings Technology Metals rise 12.9% to A$0.35

** Stock hits highest level since mid-July 2024

** The rare-earths developer to form a joint venture with Australian billionaire Andrew Forrest's Wyloo Metals for the Yangibana rare earths and niobium project in Western Australia

** Wyloo will own a 60% stake in the venture and manage and operate the project, while HAS's unit Yangibana Jubilee will hold the remaining stake

** About 1.9 million shares change hands, 19.8x the 30-day average volume

** Stock down 4.6% this year, as of last close

data.title: India 10-year yield holds at key level ahead of RBI's bond auction
data.description:
Indian government bond yields were largely unchanged on Thursday, with the benchmark bond yield near the key technical level of 6.68%, with the focus on the central bank's second open market purchase of bonds in as many weeks.

The benchmark 10-year yield (IN067934G=CC) was at 6.6820% as of 10:00 a.m. IST, compared with its previous close of 6.6859%.

The Reserve Bank of India will buy bonds worth up to 400 billion rupees ($4.61 billion) later in the day, the last leg of its mega liquidity infusion package announced in late January.

"The cutoffs would be the key deciding factor whether the benchmark bond yield will be able to break 6.68% on the downside or will reverse one more time," a trader with a primary dealership said.

The benchmark bond is part of the upcoming purchase. The RBI bought around 50 billion rupees of this paper in its first debt purchase but did not include it in the second auction.

Over the last five weeks, the central bank has bought bonds worth 600 billion rupees via auctions and another 388.15 billion rupees through secondary market screen-based purchases.

The 10-year bond yield has eased nearly 20 basis points during this period from a high of 6.87% hit on January 14.

Apart from bond buying, the RBI has also injected around 440 billion rupees through a dollar/rupee buy/sell swap and 1.82 trillion rupees through four-day to 56-day repos, as well as provided funds through daily overnight repos.

Traders also await New Delhi's penultimate auction for the current financial year and the minutes of RBI's last meeting on Friday.

Meanwhile, the minutes of the Federal Reserve's January meeting showed that U.S. President Donald Trump's initial policy proposals raised concerns about higher inflation.

($1 = 86.8170 Indian rupees)

data.title: Courage Investment Forecasts Profit for 2024
data.description: Courage Investment is expecting to book a profit of no less than $1.6 million for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, compared with a loss of around $4.0 a year earlier, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

data.title: Metaplanet Now Holds 2,100 Bitcoin, Purchases 68 More BTC
data.description:
Metaplanet (3350) has now accumulated 2,100 bitcoin (BTC), now holding 0.01% of the total BTC supply that will ever be mined, which is 21 million BTC. Metaplanet purchased 68.59 BTC for $6.6 million at an average purchase price of $96,335 per bitcoin.

According to Dylan Le Clair, Metaplanet has raised $20 million in equity capital in the first two trading days of its "21 million plan". Metaplanet also announced on Feb. 18 that they will execute a 10-1 stock split on April 1. This comes just eight months after a reverse a 1-for-10 reverse split.

Metaplanet shares are up over 1% in the current market trading; shares are trading at 6,260 JPY.

data.title: Dubai Taxi's FY24 Profit Slips; Revenue Up
data.description:
Dubai Taxi Co. (DFM:DTC) said Wednesday that its profit for full-year 2024 fell, while revenue from contracts with customers rose year over year.

Profit for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 331.3 million Emirati dirhams, compared with 345.3 million dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 0.13 dirham from 0.14 dirham.

The Dubai-listed taxi services company's revenue from contracts with customers was 2.20 billion dirhams, compared with 1.95 billion dirhams earlier.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a final dividend of 0.0489 dirham per share for the second half of 2024, payable in April 2025, subject to approval at a general assembly. The final dividend brings the total dividend amount for full-year 2024 to 0.1126 dirham per share.

data.title: Moody’s upgrades credit outlook for LG Electronics to ‘positive’
data.description: Moody’s Investors Service has upgraded LG Electronics’ credit outlook from “stable” to “positive,” the first such revision since 2021. The company’s credit rating remains at Baa2, Moody’s announced on Wednesday. Despite economic uncertainties, including rising tariffs and sluggish demand for certain products, Moody’s said that LG Electronics is expected to maintain financial stability through steady profitability and sound financial indicators. A key factor in the outlook upgrade was the credit improvement of LG Display, a subsidiary in which LG Electronics holds a 36.72 percent stake. Moody’s noted that LG Display’s improving financial position reduces the risk of contingent support from LG Electronics while also enhancing its equity method earnings. Moody’s highlighted LG Electronics’ global brand strength, market leadership, business diversification, and geographic expansion as key credit-supporting factors. It also pointed out that although it was not factored into this rating adjustment, LG Electronics’ ongoing initial public offering (IPO) of its Indian subsidiary could further strengthen its financial standing. LG Electronics filed a preliminary listing application with India’s Securities and Exchange Board at the end of 2024 and aims to sell a 15 percent stake in its Indian subsidiary via the IPO.

data.title: Correction: Auckland International Airport Posts H1 Revenue of NZ$500 Million; EPS of $0.12
data.description:
(Corrects the lede to state that the company reported earnings and the last paragraph to change the currency of the previous dividend to New Zealand dollars)

Auckland International Airport reported Thursday that its earnings per diluted share in the fiscal first half rose to NZ$0.1204, from NZ$0.0798 per diluted share a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting an EPS of NZ$0.10.

Total income for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was NZ$499.9 million, up from NZ$440.5 million in the same period last year. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected NZ$494.2 million.

The board declared an interim dividend of NZ$0.074 per share, down from the NZ$0.08 in the previously reported period, payable on April 4 to shareholders on record as of March 20.

data.title: Naver D2SF invests in self-learning autonomous driving startup
data.description: NAVER D2SF, a venture capital unit associated with Naver Corp., has invested in Whereable.ai Inc., a startup in AI-powered autonomous driving technology. Whereable.ai develops an autonomous driving solution based on self-learning AI to recognize and adapt to real-time road conditions. Unlike conventional systems that rely on high-precision maps or cloud processing, its solution uses a plug-and-play approach that allows for immediate deployment without additional infrastructure and enables flexible route and driving area adjustments based on customer needs. Whereable.ai was founded by the original members of ThorDrive, one of South Korea’s pioneering autonomous driving startups. With their extensive experience in commercializing autonomous driving technology in both Korea and the United States, the startup has a wealth of industry expertise.

data.title: Rio Tinto Posts Higher FY24 Attributable Profit; Sales Revenue Down
data.description:
Rio Tinto said Wednesday profit for full year 2024 increased while sales revenue decreased year over year.

Profit after tax attributable to owners for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was $11.55 billion, compared with $10.06 billion earlier. EPS moved to $7.072 from $6.165.

The mining giant's consolidated sales revenue was $53.66 billion, compared with $54.04 billion a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board declared a final dividend of $2.25 per share for the second half, payable April 17 to shareholders on record March 7. It paid $2.58 per share a year ago. This takes the full-year dividend to $4.02 per share, compared with $4.35 per share in the previous year.

data.title: LGES prepares for various scenarios, hinting at Trump’s tariff impacts
data.description: LG Energy Solution, Ltd. (LGES)’s chief executive hinted at potential adjustments to the battery maker’s investment plans in the United States. “We are preparing for various scenarios,” LGES CEO Kim Dong-myung said during a board meeting of the Korea Battery Industry Association on Wednesday. Kim serves as the chairman of the association that represents South Korean battery makers. He added that LGES aims to enhance investment efficiency by optimizing existing facilities rather than making new investments. When asked about Donald Trump’s proposed tariffs on vehicles, Kim refrained from offering detailed remarks, saying that it is an anticipated scenario and that the full impact would become clearer by April 2025. As the largest battery production company in the United States among its competitors, LGES is expected to be one of the main beneficiaries of Trump‘s tariff measures.

data.title: LG Innotek to begin mass production of vehicle AP modules in H2
data.description: LG Innotek Co. is set to begin mass production of its first vehicle application processor (AP) module in the second half of this year, aiming to expand its presence in the growing automotive semiconductor market. According to the company on Wednesday, it is actively promoting its vehicle AP to global semiconductor firms, particularly in North America, ahead of the scheduled production launch. LG Innotek plans to supply the module to semiconductor firms, which will integrate it into final products for global automakers. Acting as the “brain” of next-generation vehicles, the vehicle AP module is a crucial semiconductor component that controls core electronic systems and supports advanced technologies such as autonomous driving and advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS). LG Innotek’s entry into this segment is driven by rising demand for AP modules as autonomous and connected vehicles become more advanced. Traditional printed circuit board (PCB)-based semiconductor chips face limitations in handling the vast data loads generated by high-resolution digital cockpits and sophisticated ADAS features. Industry estimates predict that the global market for vehicle AP modules will grow from 33 million units in 2025 to 113 million units by 2030, expanding at an annual rate of 22 percent. A key advantage of LG Innotek’s AP module is its compact size. Measuring just 6.5 centimeters by 6.5 centimeters, the module integrates more than 400 components, including a system-on-chip (SoC) for data, graphics processing, display, and multimedia control, as well as memory semiconductors and power management integrated circuits (PMICs). The high level of integration allows automakers to reduce mainboard sizes, providing greater design flexibility. The shorter signal transmission distances between components also enhance control performance. LG Innotek plans to further enhance its AP module, improving heat dissipation performance to withstand temperatures of up to 95 degrees Celsius by the end of this year. The company will also use virtual simulations to shorten development cycles. This initiative is part of LG Innotek’s broader strategy to diversify its business portfolio beyond smartphone camera modules, which currently account for 80 percent of its entire revenue. The company is strengthening its position in the automotive sector with products such as automotive cameras, 5G-V2X communication modules, digital keys, and lighting modules. LG Innotek has introduced a 5-megapixel Red-Green-Blue (RGB)-Infrared (IR) camera for vehicles and is actively promoting it to global clients. The camera can be installed in various locations inside a car, such as rearview mirrors and passenger seats, to detect driver drowsiness, monitor attention, and check seat belt usage for passengers in both the front and second-row seats. LG Innotek’s order backlog for automotive components surpassed 13 trillion won ($9 billion) as of the end of last year. The company has also set a target to generate 5 trillion won in annual revenue from its mobility components business by 2030.

data.title: Toyota backs up LG Energy Solution with $1.5 bn order
data.description: Japan’s automaker Toyota Motor Corp. has agreed to shift a $1.5 billion order with LG Energy Solution Inc. to support operations at a Michigan battery plant after General Motors Co. backed out of the deal, according to Bloomberg. As a joint venture between LG Energy Solution and General Motors, the Lansing plant was initially designed to manufacture batteries exclusively for the latter. Since GM announced in 2024 that it would sell its stake in the facility, however, LG Energy Solution has moved to acquire full ownership in the facility. Toyota Motor and LG Energy Solution signed a contract in 2023 to supply 20 gigawatt-hours (GWh) of batteries. As part of the transition, Toyota Motor agreed to shift the 2023 order from another LG plant in Michigan to the Lansing facility, Bloomberg said, citing people familiar with the deal.

data.title: Sika Interplant Systems Gets Board Nod for Stock Split; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Sika Interplant Systems has received board approval to carry out a stock plit, according to a Wednesday filing to the BSE.

The split will convert each existing equity share with a face value of 10 rupees into five equity shares with a face value of 2 rupees each.

The company has set March 17 as the record date for the stock split.

Shares of the company were up nearly 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Seatrium Secures Drilling Unit Contract From International Maritime Industries
data.description:
Seatrium's offshore jack-up designer, Seatrium Offshore Technology, won an international tender from International Maritime Industries to supply equipment and license for a LeTourneau Super 116E Class Self-Elevating Drilling Unit, Kingdom 3.

The jack-up designs have been customized for operational requirements within the MENA region, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

Based on cyber systems, Kingdom 3 will be outfitted with 343 feet of leg and 1.5 million-pound hook load.

Seatrium's shares were down 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Field Solutions Holdings' Creditor Appoints Receivers, Managers
data.description:
Field Solutions Holdings said that Rob Smith and Matthew Hutton, partners of McGrathNicol, were appointed receivers and managers of the company and some of its units, by their secured creditor on Feb 19, according to Wednesday Australian bourse filing.

The move was made following the company's appointment of Barry Wight and Daniel Juratowitch of Cor Cordis as Voluntary Administrators, per the filing.

The receivers now have control of the company which they plan to continue to trade on a business-as-usual basis while pursuing going concern sale options or a recapitalization through the voluntary administration process, the filing said.

The company expects its shares to be suspended until further notice, the filing added.

data.title: Lenovo's Q3 revenue jumps 20% as PC industry recovers
data.description:
Lenovo on Thursday reported a 20% rise in third-quarter revenue as the global personal computer industry showed signs of recovery.

The Chinese technology company, whose main business is making and selling personal computers, reported revenue of $18.8 billion for the quarter ended December 31. That exceeded analysts' expectations of $17.82 billion, LSEG data showed.

Net profit reached $693 million compared with analysts' estimate of $367.7 million.

The global PC market is still facing uncertainty following a post-pandemic slump, with shipments growing by a modest 1.8% during the fourth quarter of 2024, according to research group IDC.

Lenovo maintained its position as the largest PC vendor for the quarter, capturing a 24.5% market share with shipments growing by 4.8%, IDC data showed.

The company has diversified beyond its core PC business in recent years, expanding into software and services. It has also positioned itself to benefit from the booming demand for artificial intelligence (AI) applications by growing its AI server and software businesses.
